data.title: Carolinas AGC leaders advocate for workforce relief on Capitol Hill in 2025, collaborating with AGC of America during NCLC.
data.description: In September 25, 2025, in Charlotte, North Carolina, five officers from the Carolinas Associated General Contractors (AGC) Board of Directors, accompanied by AGC staff, traveled to Washington, D.C. for the AGC of America's National Chapter & Leadership Conference (NCLC). During the conference, they had meetings with members of the U.S. Senate and House of Representatives.
Denmark says recent drone incursions over multiple airports were carried out by a "professional actor" in a "systematic" hybrid attack but found no evidence linking them to Russia.

data.title: Cryptocurrency market in decline, with Ethereum below $4,000. Notable stats: 134,702 traders liquidated, $427.63 million lost. Bitcoin $111,576.43, Ethereum $4,009.45, XRP $2.81, Dogecoin $0.2319. Bitcoin cycle analysis suggests nearing end or transitioning to gradual bull phase.
data.description: The cryptocurrency markets faced another decline on Thursday, with Ethereum falling below $4,000 during overnight trading. The current prices for the top cryptocurrencies are as follows: Bitcoin at $111,576.43, Ethereum at $4,009.45, Solana at $201.84, XRP at $2.81, Dogecoin at $0.2319, and Shiba Inu at $0.00001186. In the last 24 hours, 134,702 traders were liquidated, resulting in losses totaling $427.63 million, as reported by Coinglass. Data from SoSoValue indicates that spot Bitcoin ETFs saw net outflows of $241 million on Wednesday, while spot Ethereum ETFs experienced outflows of $79.4 million. Analysts, like crypto trader Jelle, have noted that Bitcoin cycles historically last around 1,064 days from the market bottom. With only about 30 days left in the current cycle, it remains uncertain whether it is approaching its end or shifting into a more extended bullish phase. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Study by ZAP Surgical Systems shows varying radiation doses in brain across radiosurgery platforms.
data.description: A company based in San Carlos, California, ZAP Surgical Systems, Inc., has introduced a new study titled “A Comparison of Whole Brain Dose During Multiple Metastases Stereotactic Radiosurgery Delivery Across Technology Platforms.” This study was published with collaboration from prominent professionals at Stanford University, Miami Cancer Institute, and others. The research focuses on the ZAP-X® Gyroscopic Radiosurgery® platform created by the company. The study compares the whole brain dose during multiple metastases stereotactic radiosurgery delivery across various technology platforms. This study features inputs from leading neurosurgeons, radiation oncologists, and medical physicists.

data.title: Capricor Therapeutics provides regulatory update on Deramiocel for DMD after FDA Type A meeting, post CRL in July 2025.
data.description: San Diego, CA - Capricor Therapeutics (NASDAQ: CAPR) revealed a recent regulatory update regarding its Biologics License Application (BLA) for Deramiocel, an investigational cell therapy targeting Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD). Following a Type A meeting with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) subsequent to receiving a Complete Response Letter (CRL) in July 2025, significant developments are underway. Capricor focuses on pioneering cell and exosome-based treatments for rare diseases. Stay tuned for further updates on this promising therapy.

data.title: Mill Creek Residential breaks ground for Modera at The Hill in Dallas, adding 387 homes and retail spaces.
data.description: A mixed-use community in Dallas will bring significant growth to a vibrant shopping center. Mill Creek Residential, known for developing top-notch rental housing, revealed plans to add 387 homes and retail spaces to the area. This move is intended to enhance the existing shopping center by providing more housing options and increasing commercial activity. The community is expected to contribute to the economic development of Dallas and create a bustling hub for residents and visitors alike.

data.title: Oracle launches AI app for agriculture data analysis at UN General Assembly, aiding in creating more resilient food systems.
data.description: In New York on September 25, 2025, during the United Nations General Assembly, an AI application was introduced. This innovative technology securely gathers and examines massive volumes of data to offer ongoing enhanced understanding of the key factors influencing food security. The aim is to strengthen food system resiliency on a global scale. The application is designed to handle vast amounts of information, accelerating the process of analyzing data for improving food security strategies. This initiative seeks to address the challenges within food systems by utilizing advanced technology to provide valuable insights that can help enhance food security measures worldwide.

data.title: Coupon interest rates for various series by Jyske Realkredit have been set from 2.14% to 3.03% p.a. until Dec 31, 2025.
data.description: Jyske Realkredit has announced the fixing of coupon interest rates for several series. The rates for Series 454.B.38 and Series 154.B.38 with ISIN DK0009361628 and DK0009361701, respectively, have been set at 3.03% p.a. for the period from 1 October 2025 to 31 March 2026. For Series 154.E.41 and Series 454.E.OA.41 with ISIN DK0009366932 and DK0009367070, the rates have been set at 2.68% p.a. For Series 422.E.OA Cb3 ju26 RF, Series 422.E.OA Cb3 ju27 RF, Series G422.E.OA Cb3 ju27 RF, Series 422.B.OA Cb3 ju27 RF, Series G422.E.OA Cb3.ju27 RF, Series 422.E.OA Cb3.ju28 RF, Series SNP322.ap.28, Series 422.E.OA Cb3.ju29 RF, and Series G422.E.OA Cb3.ju29 RF with corresponding ISINs, the rates have been set between 2.14% and 2.61% p.a. from 1 October 2025 to 31 December 2025. Inquiries can be directed to Christian Bech-Ravn, Head of Investor Relations.

data.title: US FDA QSR Compliance course on Design Controls, Verification & Validation: Oct 27-28, 2025. Focus on medical device regulations. From Business Wire via ResearchAndMarkets.com.
data.description: A training session titled "US FDA Medical Device Quality System (QSR) Compliance: Design Controls, Verification & Validation" is now available on ResearchAndMarkets.com. Taking place from October 27th to October 28th, 2025, the course offers in-depth instruction on the US FDA Quality System Regulation (QSR) pertaining to medical devices. Key topics covered include design controls, verification & validation (V&V), document management, and risk-based decision making. This training aims to ensure proper adherence to regulations for quality system compliance.

data.title: Progress Residential helps 11,000 residents set first-time credit scores through rent reporting program.
data.description: Progress Residential®, a prominent property management service for single-family rental homes based in Scottsdale, Arizona, shared the success of its no-cost positive rent reporting initiative. This program, launched in 2022, was groundbreaking in the Single-Family Rental (SFR) sector as it offers positive rent reporting to all three major credit bureaus for residents free of charge. Over 11,000 residents have benefited from this program so far, enabling them to establish initial credit scores. Additionally, thousands of others have seen enhancements in their existing credit profiles.
